mnemonics: One Arrow, One Target (OAOT): For a function, one input (arrow) can hit only one output (target). For one-to-one, one target can only be hit by one arrow.
keyPoints: A function must map every element in its domain to exactly one element in its codomain.
A function is one-to-one (injective) if distinct inputs always produce distinct outputs.
The horizontal line test is a graphical way to determine if a function is one-to-one.
commonMistakes: Confusing one-to-one functions with onto (surjective) functions. A function can be one-to-one without being onto, and vice-versa.
Assuming that if a function's rule is given by a formula (like f(x)=x²), it is automatically a function over any set. The domain and codomain are a crucial part of the definition.
